title: 4. Project Roles, Team, and Responsibilities
text_content: RACI for Design & Development defines the roles and what key responsibilities each has in the development of Instructions for Use (where Instructions for Use is used generically for GPL-owned publication types). This UAP describes GPL-relevant organization and UA-specific organization where it is not included in the DDP/SDP (or when including it in the UAP facilitates project communication). The Library CD/DVD  included for some projects is not listed in this table, as it compiles the UA. Reviewers are subject-matter experts (SME) who are integral to product development. They contribute to content development via internal reviews of drafts, and through demos and discussions. Reviewers consult and contribute (C). Formal Reviewers participate in the Formal Review phase of documents. They do not sign approval. Formal reviewers consult and contribute (C). Stakeholders have interest in the project or deliverable but are not reviewers or approvers. Stakeholders consult and contribute (C).

source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

title: 4.0 Validation Processes > 4.3 Test Case Management and Approvals
text_content: Test Cases shall be created, reviewed, and approved prior to execution per the Validation Procedure (1), ADLM Guidance (10), and RACI for Design and Development (11). Test records shall be reviewed by a second individual (Validation Manager, Validation Lead, peer Validation Specialist, Product Manager or QA). The approval of the Validation Summary Report/MRD by the Validation representative shall confirm that the test records have received this second review. The documented test process for validation consists of the Test Design Strategy and modular test cases traced to User Needs and acceptance criterion. Selected test cases may trace to more than one user need according to the clinical workflow. Each test case is designed so that the input is targeted at a high-level clinical workflow action and does not state specifically how the user must execute the action. source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.

text_content: Halcyon drawings need to be created by engineering and reviewed by manufacturing and potential supplier for DfM and cost down purpose. To reduce the effort for early EC and start purchasing early, HALv2 will release drawing as VR at beginning. Then place order to supplier. During design freeze, all drawing will be updated to RE. Once parts release to RE, planner will set up material master in SAP. According to L11284 Concurrent design transfer guide, after DFR and before DOR, changes to RE part cannot follow EC procedure. Aligned with team, all the disposition information will be added to change master. Manufacturing team will implement these changes according to information in change mater. Due to there is only 4 months from DFR to production material need arrive at factory, mfg team started early production material procurement from end of Feb 2018. text_content: This project does not require any new skills that would need new, specific training programs. Functional department managers are responsible for ensuring that their staff has appropriate and adequate qualifications and training to perform the tasks required by their job role.  The training records are managed in the Compass system. The external resources/consultants working within Varian organization will be trained adequately to understand and follow Varian Quality System and Product Life Cycle (PLC) process, and the training records will be stored in Compass. In case subprojects are outsourced to external design houses, the scope-of-work shall define the deliverables and quality controls required by Varian, so that the deliverables will meet the requirements of Varian Quality System.
